subject: Computer Networking
title: Passwords and Their Problems
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Passwords and Their Problems.

hook.text: The most common password is still '123456.' Billions of accounts are protected by guesses a child could make.

buildup.text: Passwords are hashed before storage. But weak passwords produce hashes that attackers can crack in seconds.

discovery.text: Salting adds random data before hashing. Identical passwords produce different hashes, blocking rainbow tables.

twist.text: Password reuse is the real epidemic. One breached site gives attackers credentials they try everywhere else.

climax.text: Password managers generate and store unique passwords for every site. They're the best defense against reuse.

punchline.text: A strong, unique password per site. That's the minimum.

quiz.question: Why is password reuse dangerous?
options: One breach gives attackers credentials to try on other sites
It makes passwords easier to remember
It slows down the login process
